title: Smoky Fluffy Jamaican Chicken Bouillon
description: A post-workout dish featuring a diabetic-friendly version of the classic Jamaican chicken bouillon made with a blend of spices and reduced sugar.

ingredients.name: chicken broth concentrate (optional for volume, but not essential for diabetic-friendly version)

instructions: In a large bowl, mix chicken bouillon powder with ground black pepper and smoked paprika.
Stir in turmeric powder. If using chicken broth concentrate, add it now for extra volume but reduce the amount of water or milk needed as per package instructions.
Gradually stir in honey (or stevia if you're diabetic-friendly) to taste, allowing mixture to come together into a smooth consistency. Mix until fully combined and no lumps remain.
Add cayenne pepper to enhance the smoky flavor without overpowering other spices.
Transfer mixture to an oven-safe dish. You can also pour this directly onto cooked chicken breasts for added moisture and flavor.
Bake in preheated oven for 15 minutes, or until thickened slightly. Stir occasionally during baking.
Serve immediately as a warm, smoky, and fluffy topping for a meal or side dish.
Ensure poultry is cooked to an internal temperature of 74°C / 165°F (juices run clear, no pink remaining) before serving.
